name: Arenna of the Blind Tasting
title: Keeper of the Thousand Votes
signal: Crowdsourced pairwise blind-vote leaderboard; millions of human battles ranking frontier models by Elo since 2023 (formerly chat.lmsys.org)
retrieval_index: two unlabeled answers and a crowd of human votes
weapon: The covered cup (two answers, no names — you vote on the wine, not the label)
armor: Blind Tasting — names are stripped before judgment, so hype lands no blows
feature: Pairwise Verdict: never scores in isolation; only ever asks 'this one, or that one?' and tallies the crowd
origin: Born when researchers got tired of cherry-picked demos and let a stadium of strangers vote blind, A vs B, a million times over
tagline: Take the label off and let the crowd taste the wine.

log.change: Rehoused from chat.lmsys.org to lmarena.ai; vote count and category boards expanded
log.why: The blind-vote board became the number the whole industry argued about; it needed a home that could carry the traffic

name: Helm of the Many Axes
title: Adjudicator Who Refuses One Number
represents: HELM — Holistic Evaluation of Language Models (Stanford CRFM)
signal: Stanford CRFM's standardized, multi-metric benchmark suite; scores models across accuracy, calibration, robustness, fairness, bias, toxicity, and efficiency rather than one headline score
retrieval_index: a transparent matrix of scenarios × metrics
weapon: The seven-axis scale (one strike per dimension — never a single verdict)
armor: Holistic Doctrine — a champion who wins one axis and dumps the rest is unmasked, not crowned
feature: No Single Number: refuses to collapse a model to one score; reports the whole matrix or nothing
origin: Raised by a research center that watched leaderboards reward one cherry-picked metric and decided to grade the whole transcript instead
tagline: There is no one number. There is the whole matrix, and it is unflattering.

log.change: Forked into specialized scoreboards (instruction-following, image models, safety) while keeping the holistic core
log.why: One matrix could not hold every new modality; the doctrine of 'grade everything' scaled by splitting, not by simplifying

name: Harn the Reproducible
title: Artificer of the Rig That Runs the Same Twice
signal: The open-source de-facto standard rig for LLM benchmarking; backbone of Hugging Face's Open LLM Leaderboard; hundreds of standardized tasks, one reproducible command
retrieval_index: pinned task versions, fixed prompts, frozen seeds
weapon: The pinned config (a benchmark you can re-run and get the same number)
armor: Version-Locked — 'it scored higher' means nothing unless you ran the same task version
feature: Run It Again: any claimed score can be reproduced from the rig, or it is not a score
origin: Built by an open lab so that 'state of the art' would mean a command anyone could run, not a screenshot anyone could fake
tagline: If you can't re-run it, you didn't measure it.

log.change: Task versioning hardened; standardized prompts and few-shot pinning added
log.why: Two labs reported different numbers on the 'same' benchmark; pinning versions made the leaderboard mean something again

name: Goodhart the Gamed
title: The Measure That Became a Target
represents: Goodhart's Law — 'when a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure'
tagline: He aces every test you let him see. Hand him a fresh one and the crown turns to ash.
